Version
  • Manual
  • HDF5 File Format
  • HDF5 File Format


HDF5 File Format

KWave.jl uses an HDF5 file format compatible with the k-Wave C++ binary solver. This allows using Julia for simulation setup/post-processing while offloading computation to the high-performance C++ binary.

Writing an Input File

Pass save_to_disk to kspace_first_order:

kspace_first_order(kgrid, medium, source, sensor; save_to_disk="input.h5")

This calls the following write functions in sequence:

FunctionDatasets written
write_matrixSource arrays (p_source_input, ux_source_input, …)
write_gridNx, Ny, Nz, dx, dy, dz, dt, Nt
write_flagsp_source_flag, u_source_flag, p0_source_flag, sensor_mask_type, …
write_attributescreated_by, file_description, major_version, minor_version

Running the C++ Binary

kspaceFirstOrder3D-OMP -i input.h5 -o output.h5 --p_rms --p_max

See the k-Wave C++ documentation for available flags.

Reading the Output

output = read_output("output.h5", sensor)
p_rms = output[:p_rms]

read_output maps C++ output dataset names to the same Symbol keys used by SimulationOutput.

Dataset Reference

Grid datasets (write_grid)

DatasetTypeUnits
Nx, Ny, Nzuint64grid points
dx, dy, dzfloat32m
dtfloat32s
Ntuint64steps

Flag datasets (write_flags)

DatasetValues
p_source_flag0 or 1
u_source_flag0 or 1
p0_source_flag0 or 1
sensor_mask_type1 = index, 2 = corners
nonlinear_flag0 or 1
absorbing_flag0, 1, or 2

See Also

write_matrix, write_grid, write_flags, write_attributes, read_output
